meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Build a robust SaaS starter kit to overcome version control issues. An expert-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: The absence of version control is a ticking time bomb for any freelance business. By implementing a standardized SaaS starter kit, you enforce repository initialization, automated commits, and branching discipline. This approach ensures every project is audit-ready, recoverable, and professional, turning chaotic codebases into high-velocity, scalable development environments.
content.intro:
You’re three weeks into a client project when the inevitable happens: a catastrophic merge error or a deleted file that wasn't backed up. The silence in your office is deafening as you realize the work is gone. This is the reality of operating without a strict version control workflow—it’s not just a technical oversight; it’s a direct threat to your freelance revenue.

But there is a better way. By treating your SaaS starter kit as a hardened foundation rather than just a collection of files, you can automate the guardrails that prevent these disasters. We are going to deploy an architectural blueprint that forces versioning from line one, leveraging the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to build an unshakeable development engine.

steps.title: Initialize Hardened Git Environment
steps.description: Establish a base repository with pre-configured .gitignore and branch protection policies to ensure no code enters the project without a history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized SaaS starter repository structure including .gitignore for Node/Python, a README with versioning guidelines, and a .github/workflows directory for CI/CD.

steps.title: Implement Automated Commit Hooks
steps.description: Use Husky to ensure that code cannot be committed unless it passes linting and unit tests, preventing broken states in the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks to run linting and unit tests before every commit, ensuring code quality across the starter kit.

steps.title: Define Modular Feature Slicing
steps.description: Structure your starter kit into modules to allow for granular versioning and independent development of SaaS features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current architecture into a modular feature-based folder structure, ensuring each module has its own test suite and dependency isolation.

steps.title: Automate Documentation Synthesis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate technical documentation from code comments, ensuring your version history always matches your project specs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the existing codebase and generate a comprehensive API documentation file that updates automatically on every major commit.

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Link your repository to a cloud provider with automated version tagging to ensure every deployment is traceable to a specific commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that tags the current commit version and pushes the build to a staging environment for client review.

pro_tips: Use conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:) to automatically generate changelogs for your clients, adding massive perceived value.
Never push directly to main; utilize a 'develop' branch workflow to keep your production-ready code pristine.
Implement a 'git-crypt' layer if your starter kit includes sensitive configuration templates to keep secrets out of your repo.
Keep your starter kit repo as a template on GitHub so you can spin up new projects with all versioning logic pre-baked in seconds.
Use Claude Code to periodically audit your repository for 'dead' branches or orphaned code that bloats your project history.
content.next_steps: After finalizing your starter kit, push it to a private GitHub template repository and use it as the foundation for your next five client projects to measure the efficiency gain.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Standardize your workflow, secure your code, and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
